description_long: This object is a slender, elongated bat with a rounded, bulbous handle and a long, tapered shaft. The handle appears to be made of wood, showing a natural, slightly worn brown coloration with subtle grain patterns and some signs of use. The shaft is metallic, transitioning from the handle with a slightly rusted or weathered appearance, and ends in a flat, chisel-like tip rather than a traditional rounded barrel. The overall finish is matte, with the wood showing a smooth but slightly grippy texture, while the metal shaft is harder and more reflective. There are no visible logos, grip tape, or decorative markings on the handle or shaft. The end of the handle is rounded and unadorned, matching the rest of the wooden grip.
description: A slender bat with a wooden handle and a long, metallic shaft, featuring a flat tip and a worn, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bat, highlighting the wooden handle and the long, metallic shaft.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the flat, chisel-like tip and the rounded handle end-on.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the length and the transition between the wooden handle and metal shaft.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the wooden handle.
